meta.title: Tha Phae Gate (ประตูท่าแพ) | East Gate of Chiang Mai Old City · Free · Open 24 Hours
meta.description: Tha Phae Gate (ประตูท่าแพ) is the eastern gate of Chiang Mai's Old City, first built when King Mangrai founded the walled city in 1296 and rebuilt in the 1980s. Free and open 24 hours, it is Chiang Mai's living room, a pigeon square and the start of the Sunday Walking Street. Rated 4.4 on Google (29,240 reviews).
meta.keywords: Tha Phae Gate, ประตูท่าแพ, Chiang Mai Old City, Chiang Mai landmark, Sunday Walking Street, things to do in Chiang Mai, Chiang Mai travel

header.siteName: Tha Phae Gate (ประตูท่าแพ)
header.sub: The eastern gateway of Chiang Mai's Old City · a civic square and festival ground

nonprofitBanner.text: This is a non-commercial guide to Tha Phae Gate, compiled by volunteers to help independent travelers plan their visit. For reference only — please rely on on-site and official information.

mission.title: About this guide
mission.text: We are volunteers who love Chiang Mai. Our aim is to introduce Tha Phae Gate — its history, transport and how to visit — clearly, accurately and ad-free, in Chinese, English and Thai, so every visit is a little easier.

legend.quote: If Chiang Mai has a 'gateway living room', it must be Tha Phae Gate — more than a gate, it is the eastern heartbeat of the old city.

tags: East Gate of the Old City

factsSource.label: Source: Chiang Mai municipality & public travel info

sunsetPlanner.title: Different moods by day and night
sunsetPlanner.note: Tha Phae Gate is open all day. Early morning is quiet with soft light, great for photos; after dark the square lights up and the Sunday Night Market comes alive.
sunsetPlanner.photoTip: 🌅 The gate faces east — side-light from 6–8 am is best for the wall and pigeons; the blue hour after sunset is the golden window for night shots.
sunsetPlanner.weatherTip: ☀️ March–May is hot; bring sun protection and water. June–Oct is rainy season with afternoon showers.

quickFactsDashboard.title: Tha Phae Gate · At a glance
quickFactsDashboard.intro: Everything you need to know about this Chiang Mai landmark at a glance.

items.label: Gate type
items.desc: One of the five main gates planned when King Mangrai walled the city in 1296; the present structure was rebuilt in the 1980s from historical records.

items.label: Founded
items.desc: Built with the moat and walls when King Mangrai (Mangrai) founded Chiang Mai; the current gate was rebuilt in 1985–1986.

items.label: District
items.desc: Midway along the eastern wall of the Old City; outside the gate is Tha Phae Road, leading east to the night bazaar and the Ping River.

items.label: Landmark type
items.desc: Both a historic monument and a civic gathering place, photo spot and festival venue.

items.label: Coordinates
items.desc: Plus Code: QXQV+38 Chiang Mai. Locatable directly on Google Maps.

items.label: Admission
items.desc: An open public square, no ticket, accessible any time; nearby temples charge separately.

hiddenRules.title: Tips for a comfortable visit
hiddenRules.subtitle: A few small habits make your Old City trip easier.

items.label: Shoes & dress
items.text: The square and old town are open-air; wear comfortable shoes. Cover shoulders and knees when entering nearby temples.

items.label: Weather & sun
items.text: Chiang Mai is sunny and hot by afternoon; bring a hat, sunscreen and water. June–Oct is rainy season — carry an umbrella.

items.label: Pigeons & photos
items.text: The square's pigeons are a highlight; buy feed to interact and photograph, but don't chase them. Drones must follow local rules.

items.label: Courtesy & volume
items.text: This is a public square near temples; keep your voice down and respect the old walls.

items.label: Litter & cleanliness
items.text: Take your rubbish with you to protect the old town; sort waste, especially on market nights.

items.label: Safety & belongings
items.text: Crowds are dense — watch your phone and bag. It is safe at night, but stay aware.

complianceGuide.title: Visit respectfully · Protect the old city together
complianceGuide.subtitle: Tha Phae Gate is Chiang Mai's public space; a few simple manners help everyone enjoy it.

rules.title: Respect religious sites
rules.text: Nearby temples are places of worship — remove shoes, cover shoulders and knees, don't shout or climb on Buddha images.

rules.title: Animal protection & laws
rules.text: While the pigeon square is a photo hotspot, feeding pigeons is strictly prohibited by law (violators face heavy fines). Pigeon droppings are acidic and severely corrode the ancient red-brick walls. Please do not buy feed or feed them, helping protect both ecology and heritage.

rules.title: Heritage protection
rules.text: The ancient walls and gates are irreplaceable historical relics. Please do not climb, lean on, or vandalize the red brick walls. Maintain a respectful distance when taking photos and watch for warning signs.

rules.title: Be considerate
rules.text: Crowds at the square, crossings and night market are dense — yield to pedestrians, follow on-site guidance, and look after the elderly and children.

higashiyamaRoute.title: Tha Phae Gate · Half-day walking route
higashiyamaRoute.subtitle: Starting at the gate, spend half a day exploring the eastern half of Chiang Mai's Old City.

steps.title: Tha Phae Gate square
steps.text: Begin at the gate and pigeon square; morning light is softest. Read the info boards to understand the old city layout.

steps.title: North along the east wall
steps.text: Follow the moat and wall northward to feel the square plan of the Lanna old city and its water system.

steps.title: Three Kings Monument
steps.text: A bronze monument to the three kings who founded Chiang Mai — the geographic heart of the old town.

steps.title: Wat Chedi Luang & Wat Phra Singh
steps.text: Two of Chiang Mai's most important Lanna temples; visit the great stupa and scripture library nearby.

steps.title: Back to Tha Phae Rd · Sunday Market
steps.text: Return to the gate area in the evening; on Sundays, stroll the famous Sunday Walking Street for food and crafts.

higashiyamaRoute.disclaimer: About 3 km total, mostly flat. The Sunday Walking Street closes Tha Phae Road from ~16:00 on Sundays — go early to avoid crowds.

intro.title: What is Tha Phae Gate?
intro.description: Tha Phae Gate (ประตูท่าแพ) sits on the eastern wall of Chiang Mai's Old City and is its most representative gate — today also the city's living room. 'Tha Phae' means 'raft pier' in Thai: the gate historically faced the Ping River, where teak rafts landed to trade. Today the gate is free and open all day; its square is a hub for feeding pigeons, photos, gatherings and festivals, and the starting point of the Sunday Walking Street.

officialManagement.title: Management & on-site services
officialManagement.text: The square is managed by the Chiang Mai City Municipality (เทศบาลนครเชียงใหม่) as public space — daily opening, maintenance and festival setup are coordinated by them; info boards and volunteers offer basic guidance. Nearby temples are managed by the monasteries and the Fine Arts Department.

alsoKnownAs.title: You may also hear it called
items: The 'East Gate' of Chiang Mai: one of the five main gates, the eastern link between the old city and the outside (night bazaar, Ping River).
Home of the living room and the pigeons: an open square with year-round pigeons, a favorite rest, photo and meeting spot for visitors and locals.
Start of the Sunday Walking Street: every Sunday evening, Tha Phae Road closes west from the gate into a pedestrian market — one of Chiang Mai's most famous events.

transportSection.title: Getting to Tha Phae Gate · Transport
transportSection.subtitle: The gate is on the east side of the Old City, about 7 km from Chiang Mai International Airport; walking, red songthaew and Grab are all easy within the old town.
transportSection.overviewText: Tha Phae Gate is among the easiest Chiang Mai landmarks to reach: a 15–20 min taxi from the airport; the old town is compact and most sights are walkable.
transportSection.overviewText2: The gate itself is an open square with no ticket booth or fence; the Tha Phae Road / Old City Road junction nearby is the old town's transport hub.
arrivalItems: From Chiang Mai International (CNX) by taxi or Grab straight to the east gate.
Within the old town, hop on a red songthaew — wave to stop, hop on and off.
Stay near the old town or night bazaar and walk to most temples and markets.

modes.label: Flight + transfer
modes.title: Airport to old town
modes.description: Chiang Mai International is about 7 km from the gate — the entry point for most international and domestic travelers.
details: Airport shuttle / Grab / taxi reach the east gate in about 15–20 minutes.
Late-night arrivals are easy too; the gate area is reachable 24 hours.

steps.t: Use Grab or the airport taxi counter after arrivals.
Get off and walk into the square; signs are posted along the way.

modes.label: Songthaew
modes.title: Red Songthaew
modes.description: The most convenient transport in and around the old town, priced by distance, great for short hops.
details: Wave to stop and tell the driver 'Tha Phae Gate'.
Handy for the old town, night market and nearby temples.

modes.label: Grab / taxi
modes.title: Ride-hailing
modes.description: Book a Grab straight to the gate — transparent pricing and accurate location.
details: Easy from the night market, riverside or your hotel.
Book a bit earlier during peak (night-market evenings).

modes.label: Walk / bike
modes.title: Slow travel
modes.description: The old town is compact; from the gate most temples and markets are walkable.
details: Bike rental is available in the old town; riding the moat path is pleasant.
On Sunday market nights Tha Phae Road is pedestrian-only — walking is best.

parking.title: Parking info
parking.note: The gate is an open square with no dedicated parking; drivers can use nearby commercial lots and walk in.

options.name: Commercial lots along Tha Phae Rd

parking.disclaimer: Prices are estimates — follow on-site signs. During festivals and market nights spaces are tight; arrive early or use transit.

localTips: Early morning and late afternoon have the fewest crowds and the best light for photos.
Cafés, convenience stores and ATMs around the square make resupply easy.
On Sunday market nights, use the gate as your start and walk west along the street.

bestTime.title: Best time to visit
bestTime.text: For photos choose 6–9 am; for festivals and market atmosphere, Sunday evening to night. Open all day, no booking needed.

driverFaq.q: Can I drive right up to the gate?
driverFaq.a: The square is pedestrian-only; vehicles cannot enter. Park nearby and walk in.

driverFaq.q: Is parking easy in the old town?
driverFaq.a: Spaces are limited; use public lots or songthaew / Grab for transfers.

officialManagement.title: Management & on-site services
officialManagement.text: The square is managed by the Chiang Mai City Municipality (เทศบาลนครเชียงใหม่) as public space — daily opening, maintenance and festival setup are coordinated by them; info boards and volunteers offer basic guidance. Nearby temples are managed by the monasteries and the Fine Arts Department.

humanities.title: The culture beyond the gate
humanities.subtitle: Tha Phae Gate is more than a gate — it is a key to understanding the Lanna old city.

sections.title: 1296 · Founding of Lanna & the walls
sections.content: King Mangrai founded Chiang Mai in 1296 and built the moat and walls. Tha Phae Gate was originally known as 'Chiang Rueak Gate'. 'Tha Phae' means 'raft pier', reflecting its historical role as a water trade hub connecting to the Ping River. It served as the main eastern entrance to the Old City.

cards.title: King Mangrai
cards.text: Founder of the Lanna kingdom, who planned Chiang Mai's square city and moat system.

cards.title: Moat & walls
cards.text: The square moat (คูเมือง) and earthen-stone walls formed the city's defensive core.

cards.title: Five main gates
cards.text: The old city had east, west, south, north gates; Tha Phae was the eastern portal.

sections.title: 16th–18th c. · War & change
sections.content: During the Burmese Toungoo occupation (from 1558), walls and gates saw repeated fighting and repair; as the capital's focus shifted, parts of the wall fell into ruin.

fieldNote.title: What to see
fieldNote.text: Remaining wall sections can still be traced along the moat path — tangible evidence of the city's changes.

sections.title: 1985–1986 · Reconstruction
sections.content: The current red-brick gate is not the original 1296 structure. In 1985, to prepare for Chiang Mai's 700th anniversary, the Fine Arts Department reconstructed the ruined gate based on old photographs and historical archives. This greatly enhanced its documentary and visual value as a historical landmark.

academicNote.title: On the dates
academicNote.text: The present gate is a late-20th-century reconstruction; the original walls are older, and exact rebuild years vary by source.

sections.title: Modern · Civic square & festival portal
sections.content: Tha Phae Gate is more than an ancient monument; it is Chiang Mai's 'living room'. It serves as the core stage for major public festivals: the main battleground of Songkran, the starting point of the Loy Krathong parade, and the portal to the famous Sunday Night Market.

sections.title: Belief · Lanna astrology & guardian spirits
sections.content: The eight gates of Chiang Mai's old city (including the corner bastions) closely correspond to Lanna astrology (Bagua). Each gate houses a specific guardian spirit. Tha Phae Gate is not only a physical defense but also a spiritual boundary, allowing visitors to feel the site's profound cultural significance.

sections.title: Festivals · Market & tradition
sections.content: During Songkran (Thai New Year water festival), Loy Krathong, Yi Peng (lantern festival) and the Flower Festival, the area around the gate hosts major celebrations; every Sunday is the famous Walking Street.

cards.title: Sunday Walking Street
cards.text: Every Sunday evening, a pedestrian market stretching west from the gate — a signature Chiang Mai experience.

cards.title: Songkran
cards.text: The Thai New Year water festival; the gate area is a core celebration zone of the old city.

cards.title: Loy Krathong / Yi Peng
cards.text: Season of floating krathongs and sky lanterns; the old city and moat glow with light.

sections.title: Imagery · Gate & old city
sections.content: 'Tha Phae' means raft pier, echoing the city's river-born history; the gate, moat and walls together form the complete experience of Chiang Mai's Old City.

didYouKnow.title: Did you know?
didYouKnow.text: The Thai 'ท่าแพ' literally means 'raft pier' — because the gate historically faced the Ping River, where teak rafts floated down to land and trade.

sitePlaques.title: On-site plaques · Read the gate
sitePlaques.subtitle: Bilingual Thai–English plaques stand near the gate; compare them on the spot.
sitePlaques.intro: Tap a card to read the Chinese explanation.

items.body: Explains the gate's role as the eastern gate of Chiang Mai's Old City and why it was rebuilt in the 1980s from historical records.

items.koTitle: ถนนคนเดินท่าแพ
items.title: Sunday Walking Street plaque
items.body: Marks the pedestrian market stretching west from the gate every Sunday evening — one of Chiang Mai's signature events.

items.koTitle: ลานประตูท่าแพ
items.title: Civic square plaque
items.body: Explains the square's contemporary role as a place for citizens to rest, gather, feed pigeons and hold festivals.

gallery.subtitle: From dawn to night, see the many faces of this civic living room.

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.subtitle: Transport, history, visiting and surroundings — cleared up first.

items.q: Are there restrooms at the gate?
items.a: The square is open with no dedicated restroom; use nearby cafés, convenience stores and malls.

items.q: Can I drive to the gate? Is parking easy?
items.a: Vehicles cannot enter the square; park at nearby commercial lots and walk in. Spaces in the old town are limited — prefer transit.
The square and ground are flat with no steps; wheelchairs and strollers move easily, with good sidewalk and crossing connections.
Cafés and convenience stores along Tha Phae Road offer resupply, charging and rest.
Thai 'ท่าแพ' means raft pier — the gate historically faced the Ping River, where teak rafts landed to trade.
Chiang Mai was founded by King Mangrai in 1296 with walls; the present gate was rebuilt in the 1980s from records.

items.q: What is the gate's relation to the old city?
items.a: It is the eastern gate; outside is Tha Phae Road, leading east to the night bazaar and Ping River — the eastern portal of the old city.

items.q: Is there an entrance fee? What are the hours?
items.a: Free and open 24 hours; no booking. Nearby temples charge separately.

items.q: How long should I plan for?
items.a: A quick stop is 20–30 min; with a half-day old-city walk and Sunday market, plan half a day.

items.q: When is the best time to go?
items.a: Early morning has few crowds and good light for photos; Sunday evening to night has the Walking Street at its liveliest.

items.q: What else is a must nearby?
items.a: Wat Chedi Luang, Wat Phra Singh and the Three Kings Monument inside the old city, plus the night bazaar and Ping River east of Tha Phae Road.

items.q: How to enjoy the Sunday Market?
items.a: From ~16:00 every Sunday, Tha Phae Road closes west from the gate into a pedestrian street — start at the gate and head west.
The flat square and pigeon feeding are family-friendly; just watch sun protection and hydration.

scienceArt.title: Architecture & symbolism
scienceArt.subtitle: The gate, moat and walls together form the complete experience of Chiang Mai's Old City.

scienceMuseum.name: The form of Tha Phae Gate
scienceMuseum.highlight: As the eastern gate, its solid brick-stone arch and gatehouse echo the Lanna wall's defensive vocabulary — a tangible entry point to understanding the old city.
points: The arched gateway is the main passage form for city traffic.
The present structure continues the old wall system, rebuilt from 20th-c. records.
With the square moat and walls, it forms the city's defense and boundary.

artMuseum.name: Gate & daily life
artMuseum.highlight: From the historic 'raft pier' imagery to today's pigeon square and festival stage, Tha Phae Gate is where Chiang Mai blends history with daily life.
points: Its name carries the memory of a river-born city.
The square is a public place for citizens to gather, feed pigeons and photograph.
During festivals it becomes a city stage linking tradition and the present.

outdoorMuseum.title: Photo & spotting spots
outdoorMuseum.subtitle: No need to go far — the gate area has great angles.

items.name: Morning side-light
items.location: Square in front of the gate
items.background: 6–8 am has the fewest people; eastern side-light hits the wall — clean portraits and architecture.
points: Low angle with pigeons as foreground

items.name: Pigeon square
items.background: Buy feed to interact and shoot — a relaxed favorite for families and couples.

items.name: Blue hour
items.background: After sunset the sky turns blue and the gate lights up, contrasting warm and cool with the street.

items.name: Wall & moat
items.background: Along the moat path you can shoot the wall, water and greenery — layered and quiet.

education.title: Why Tha Phae Gate is worth a visit
education.subtitle: It is both a landmark and an entry point to understanding Chiang Mai.

points.title: A living specimen of the old city
points.text: The gate, walls and moat preserve the spatial logic of the Lanna founding — the first step to reading Chiang Mai.

points.title: Free and open 24 hours
points.text: As an open public square, no ticket and no time limit — friendly to any budget and pace.
items: Great first stop or transit point

points.title: Where festival meets daily life
points.text: From the Sunday Walking Street to Songkran, the gate links Chiang Mai's traditional festivals with civic daily life.

plantGuide.title: Chiang Mai weather & best season
plantGuide.subtitle: Pick the right time and your gate-and-old-city trip is more comfortable.

comparison.name: Nov – Feb
comparison.summary: The most comfortable time of year: cool, dry and blue skies — peak for old-city walks and festivals.

comparison.name: Jun – Oct
comparison.summary: Afternoon showers are usually brief; the greenest season with fewer crowds and friendlier prices.

plantGuide.seasonSubtitle: Three seasons, each with its own flavor — plan to your preference.

seasons.name: Cool (Nov–Feb)
seasons.summary: The pleasant season; a slight day-night temperature gap but overall comfortable.

seasons.name: Hot (Mar–May)
seasons.summary: Strong sun and high heat; avoid midday, hydrate and use sun protection.

seasons.name: Rainy (Jun–Oct)
seasons.summary: Mostly showers, lush green, fewer crowds.

audience.title: Who is it for?
audience.subtitle: Whatever kind of traveler you are, Tha Phae Gate has something for you.

items.resonance: Morning and blue-hour walls, pigeons and night views are all very photogenic.
items.tip: Bring a wide-angle and tripod; claim a spot early on Sunday market nights.

items.resonance: The gate, walls and plaques string together a condensed history of the Lanna founding.
stops: Wat Chedi Luang / Wat Phra Singh
items.tip: Read alongside the bilingual Thai–English plaques on site for deeper understanding.

items.resonance: A flat square, pigeon interaction and snacks keep kids happy too.
items.tip: Watch sun protection and hydration; supervise kids when feeding pigeons.

items.resonance: As the eastern gate and transport hub, it is an ideal first stop for planning your trip.
items.tip: Treat it as the 'old-city portal' and expand outward from there.

lodging.title: Where to stay nearby
lodging.subtitle: With the gate as the center, three areas each have a focus.
lodging.scenarioTitle: Pick an area by your pace

options.name: Inside the Old City
options.highlight: Walk to the gate and main temples; quiet alleys with character.
options.commute: Most hotels are a 10–20 min walk to the gate.

options.name: Night bazaar / Tha Phae Rd
options.highlight: Next to the Sunday Market and commercial area; food and shopping downstairs.

options.name: Ping River / riverside
options.highlight: East toward the Ping River; riverside hotels and morning markets have their own charm.
options.commute: About 10 min by taxi to the gate.

lodging.peak: Cool season (Nov–Feb) and around Songkran are Chiang Mai's busiest; old-town and night-bazaar stays fill up — book early.
tips: For walkable access, pick stays tagged 'Old City' or 'Tha Phae Road'.
Families with elders/kids may prefer hotels with a pool and elevator.
On Sunday market nights, staying in the old city lets you walk back easily.
